failure_modes.text: Surface analogy can make unlike systems look equivalent just because they share one visible institution or label.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge surface analogies by mapping the underlying institutional mechanisms; shared labels can mask fundamentally different causal structures.

failure_modes.text: Inside-view or motivated reasoning can make analysts interpret cross-system evidence through a preferred domestic frame.
failure_modes.source_quote: interpret cross-system evidence in ways that confirm their preferred political framework
failure_modes.mitigation: Discipline inside-view bias by requiring at least one alternative interpretation grounded in the other system's own logic, not just the domestic frame.

failure_modes.text: System maps can become so sprawling that everything connects to everything and no actionable comparison remains.
failure_modes.mitigation: Limit the system map to actionable comparison dimensions; if everything connects to everything, the analysis has lost its diagnostic leverage.

premortem_questions.text: What structural difference between these systems would most likely break the analogy we are currently relying on?
premortem_questions.source_quote: formal rules and informal behavior interact across different system designs

premortem_questions.text: Which make-or-break institutional assumption are we importing from one regime that should be tested instead?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: If the outside view on comparable systems is sparse, are we mistaking uncertainty for stable probability?
premortem_questions.source_quote: sparse comparables are treated as stable probabilities

heuristics.text: Compare systems through institutional structure, incentives, and power interactions instead of surface narrative.
Use system mapping and causal analysis to separate machine-level patterns from case-level events.
Run explicit comparative hypotheses so cross-system evidence can falsify a preferred interpretation.

curation_notes.summary: Chunk set stays close to the source's strongest failure modes, premortem questions, and repeatable heuristics.
donor_drops: Did not widen the chunk surface beyond clearly source-backed failure, premortem, and heuristic material.
open_questions: Some chunk text is compressed for operational reuse, but each item stays traceable to explicit source language.

allies: Relation-rich ally material was left for Wave 3 instead of being smuggled into chunk text.
antagonists: Bias and conflict material stronger as first-order relations was deferred to Wave 3.
structured_tensions: Any higher-order candidacy remains observational only in batch artifacts.
